title: carex tetanica var. woodii
intro: Carex tetanica var. woodii, also known as Wood's sedge or wood sedge, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae) and is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Carex tetanica var. woodii include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat skin conditions, and support respiratory health. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
